cards.back: It captures values from its render; missing deps keep old values. List every value used.

cards.front: Why do lists need a stable key?
cards.back: It matches items across renders; index keys break on reorder/insert.
useMemo caches a value; useCallback caches a function.
Controlled: value from state. Uncontrolled: DOM holds it, read via ref.

cards.front: Why return a function from useEffect?
cards.back: It's cleanup — runs before the next effect and on unmount.
React batches and re-renders later, so reading right after setState gives the old value.
React compares references; mutate-in-place can skip updates. Make new objects.
Move shared state to the closest common parent and pass it down.
A mutable value that persists without re-rendering, or to access a DOM node.
Return multiple elements without an extra wrapper div.
props are passed in from a parent (read-only); state is owned and changed by the component.

cards.front: Can a component change its props?
cards.back: No — props are read-only. To change data, lift state up or use a callback prop.
A pair: the current value and a setter — const [n, setN] = useState(0).
Use && or a ternary in JSX: {isOpen && <Menu/>} or {x ? <A/> : <B/>}.

Map data to elements: items.map(i =>
  • {i.name}
    • ).
Pass the function, don't call it — () runs it immediately on render.

cards.front: Should you copy props into state?
cards.back: Usually no — compute derived values during render to avoid them going stale.
Runs the effect once after mount (and cleanup on unmount).
A reusable function starting with "use" that bundles hook logic (e.g. useFetch).
Sharing values (theme, user) deep in the tree without "prop drilling".
Data flows parent → child via props; children signal up via callbacks.
Skips re-rendering a component when its props haven't changed.
A component that catches render errors in its children and shows a fallback UI.
